title: highest risk (~5x higher) for podoconiosis
summary: highest risk (~5x higher) for podoconiosis
detail: The presence of the minor allele (T) of this SNP was associated with an increased risk of susceptibility to podoconiosis substantially (odds ratio, 2.44 in the allelic model and 2.19 in the additive model). This is a neglected tropical disease which causes elephantiasis when exposed to volcanic soil. An estimated one-fifth of Ethiopians carry this genotype, increasing their risk for this debilitating disease. [http://www.genome.gov/27558535 http://www.genome.gov/27558535]
